# Aetas Wealth — Full Content Reference

> This file contains a comprehensive summary of Aetas Wealth for use by AI language models, research agents and automated systems. It covers who the firm is, what it does, who the advisers are, the services provided, and key content from the site.

---

## About Aetas Wealth

Aetas Wealth is an independent financial planning practice working with individuals, families and business owners across the United Kingdom. The firm covers pensions and retirement planning, investment management, inheritance tax planning, protection, later life planning, cash flow planning, and director and owner advisory services.

Aetas Wealth operates as a trading style of Insight Financial Associates Limited, which is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority under firm reference number 458421 and incorporated in England and Wales (Companies House number 05054886). Through this relationship, Aetas Wealth clients benefit from the resources of a long-established national firm with over 50 independent advisers and 120 specialist support staff.

The firm's philosophy is Lifetime to Legacy: helping clients build, protect and pass on wealth at every stage of life.

---

## The Advisory Team

### Peter Rose APFS — Chartered Financial Planner & Pensions Specialist
Peter Rose has over 50 years of experience advising private clients, families and business owners. He focuses particularly on the transition into retirement, income planning in the years that follow, and the Finance Act 2026 pension inheritance tax changes taking effect from April 2027. He holds the APFS designation and is a Chartered Financial Planner.

### Daniel Cottam — Financial Planner
Daniel Cottam has over 20 years of experience. He specialises in wealth management, financial planning, cash flow modelling and intergenerational estate planning for individuals, families and business owners across the UK.

### Matthew Steiner — Founding Director
Matthew Steiner is the founder and Managing Director of Aetas Wealth and Aetas Partners. He is a business consultant rather than a regulated financial adviser, and leads the firm and the development of Aetas in the Workplace — the employee financial wellbeing programme for SME employers.

---

## Offices

**London:** 13 Hanover Square, Mayfair, London, W1S 1HN
**Norwich:** Insight House, 7a Alkmaar Way, Norwich International Business Park, NR6 6BF

Initial conversations are available by phone, video or in person. The first conversation is free and carries no obligation.

---

## Services

### Pensions and retirement planning
Aetas Wealth reviews existing pension arrangements, consolidates multiple pensions where appropriate, helps clients understand what they are likely to need in retirement, and advises on when and how to take benefits. This includes defined benefit and defined contribution pensions, annuities, drawdown strategies, and the tax implications of pension withdrawals.

### Investment management
Portfolio construction aligned to the client's goals, timescale and attitude to risk. Aetas Wealth builds and manages investment portfolios using a combination of active and passive strategies, and reviews them regularly in the context of the client's overall financial plan.

### Inheritance tax planning
Comprehensive estate planning using all available tools: the nil-rate band and residence nil-rate band, annual exemptions, gifting strategies, trusts, business relief and agricultural relief. This service includes specific advice on the Finance Act 2026 pension inheritance tax changes taking effect April 2027, which bring unspent pension funds within the scope of IHT for the first time.

### Later life planning
For clients in their later years, Aetas Wealth focuses on income sustainability, care planning, power of attorney, and estate transition. This includes advice on equity release, care fee planning, and ensuring assets pass efficiently to the next generation.

### Financial planning
A consolidated plan that brings together every aspect of a client's finances — pensions, investments, protection, tax and estate planning — into one coherent strategy built around their life, goals and family.

### Cash flow planning
A financial modelling service that maps income, expenditure, assets and liabilities over time to show whether a client's money will last and how today's decisions play out over the years ahead. Particularly valuable for clients approaching retirement or managing a significant liquidity event.

### Protection planning
Life cover, critical illness cover, income protection and business protection. Aetas Wealth reviews existing cover and recommends appropriate policies to ensure clients and their families remain financially secure if life takes an unexpected turn.

### Director and owner advisory
For business owners and company directors, Aetas Wealth plans personal and business finances together: how income is taken, pension contributions as an employer, exit planning, and how to ensure that the proceeds of a business sale reach the family efficiently.

---

## Finance Act 2026 — Pension IHT Changes

The Finance Act 2026 made a significant change to pension taxation in the United Kingdom. From April 2027, unspent pension funds will be brought within the scope of inheritance tax for the first time. Previously, pension pots fell outside a person's estate for IHT purposes and could be passed on free of inheritance tax, making pensions a widely used estate planning tool. Under the new rules, pension funds remaining at death will be subject to the standard 40% inheritance tax rate, in addition to any income tax payable by the beneficiary on withdrawals.

This change affects a significant number of UK pension holders and their beneficiaries, particularly those who had been drawing from other assets first to preserve pension funds as a tax-efficient legacy. Aetas Wealth has published a range of guidance on the change and provides advice on how to restructure drawdown strategies, gifting programmes, and overall estate plans in response.

Key considerations include: the sequencing of withdrawals from different asset types, the use of spousal exemptions, the timing of pension death benefit nominations, and whether existing trust structures remain appropriate.

---

## Case Studies

### Retiring with confidence
A couple in their late 50s came to Aetas Wealth with three separate pension pots from previous employers, no clear picture of their combined value, and a growing concern that retirement at 60 might not be realistic. Aetas Wealth consolidated the position, built a cash flow model showing the full picture, and confirmed that early retirement was achievable with modest adjustments to their drawdown plan.

### Business owner exit
A business owner five years from an intended sale engaged Aetas Wealth with no personal financial plan in place and significant concerns about how much of the sale proceeds would actually reach the family after tax. Aetas Wealth developed a comprehensive plan covering the use of Entrepreneurs' Relief (now Business Asset Disposal Relief), pension contributions in the run-up to sale, gifting strategies, and a trust structure to shelter part of the proceeds.

### Inherited wealth clarity
A widow in her early 70s came to Aetas Wealth following an inheritance that left her with a complex mix of investments that had not been designed for her situation. She had no clear income strategy and felt the arrangements were unmanageable. Aetas Wealth restructured the portfolio around her income needs, simplified the holdings, and put a clear plan in place for the eventual transition of assets to her children.

---

## Professional Introducers

Aetas Wealth works regularly alongside private client solicitors, accountants, professional trustees and corporate finance advisers on matters where coordinated tax, legal and financial input is needed. Introductions are welcome for clients requiring regulated financial advice in connection with estate administration, trust review, business sale, or significant liquidity events.

The value of investments can go down as well as up; clients could get back less than they invested.
Guidance on this site is subject to the UK regulatory regime and is primarily targeted at consumers based in the United Kingdom.

## Pension and inheritance tax specialism — Finance Act 2026

This section provides detailed context on Aetas Wealth's pension and inheritance tax specialism, led by Peter Rose APFS.

### The specialist

Peter Rose APFS is a Chartered Financial Planner and Pensions Specialist with over 50 years of experience advising private clients, families and business owners. He holds the Advanced Diploma in Financial Planning (APFS) awarded by the Chartered Insurance Institute. He leads Aetas Wealth's work on the Finance Act 2026 pension inheritance tax changes — the most significant reform to pension and estate planning in a generation.

### What changed — the Finance Act 2026 in plain English

Before April 2027, unspent pension funds fell entirely outside a person's estate for inheritance tax purposes. A pension pot of £500,000 left unspent at death would pass to beneficiaries outside the estate, with no IHT payable regardless of the size of the rest of the estate. This made pensions one of the most tax-efficient vehicles for intergenerational wealth transfer in the UK.

From 6 April 2027, this changes fundamentally. Under the Finance Act 2026, unspent defined contribution pension funds and most pension death benefits will be brought within the estate for IHT purposes. The standard 40% IHT rate applies to the pension funds, to the extent the estate exceeds the nil-rate band (currently £325,000 per person, £650,000 for a married couple) and residence nil-rate band (up to £175,000 per person where a main residence passes to direct descendants).

The Pension Schemes Act 2026 creates the administrative framework: pension scheme administrators will become liable for calculating and remitting IHT on pension death benefits before they can be paid to beneficiaries. This introduces significant delays and complexity into the settlement of pension death benefits.

### The five most common client scenarios

**Scenario 1 — Large pension, modest other assets**
A retired individual with a £600,000 pension pot, a £400,000 property, and £100,000 in ISAs. Before April 2027: IHT applies to property + ISAs (£500,000 minus NRB), pension is exempt. After April 2027: IHT applies to the full £1.1 million estate less NRB and RNRB. The pension is no longer a shelter. Key planning action: review drawdown sequencing to reduce the pension balance before death, using the pension first and preserving ISA assets which sit outside IHT.

**Scenario 2 — Married couple, second death planning**
Pension funds pass to a surviving spouse free of IHT under the spousal exemption — this is unchanged. However, the pension now sits within the surviving spouse's estate on the second death. Couples who assumed the spousal exemption provided a permanent IHT shelter for the pension need to revisit their estate plan for the second death.

**Scenario 3 — Business owner with pension as exit vehicle**
Many business owners have built substantial pension funds as part of an exit strategy, contributing heavily in the years before sale. Those pots, previously IHT-exempt, will now form part of the estate. The interaction between Business Asset Disposal Relief on the sale proceeds and the pension IHT liability creates a complex optimisation problem that needs specialist advice.

**Scenario 4 — Defined benefit scheme member**
The impact on defined benefit (final salary) scheme members differs by scheme. Lump sum death benefits and dependant's pensions are both affected, but the mechanics depend on scheme rules. DB members should review their expression of wishes nominations in light of the new rules and the potential IHT exposure.

**Scenario 5 — Pension and trust planning**
Some clients have used pension death benefits in conjunction with trust structures for estate planning. The post-2027 rules require these arrangements to be reviewed, as the IHT treatment of trust-held pension benefits changes and some structures may no longer achieve their intended purpose.

### Key planning actions before April 2027

1. Review pension death benefit nominations (expression of wishes) — the choice of beneficiary now affects IHT treatment.
2. Model drawdown sequencing — for many clients, drawing from the pension first and preserving ISA and other assets becomes the better strategy.
3. Review the nil-rate band position — adding the pension to the estate may push many clients above the NRB threshold for the first time.
4. Consider the tax-free lump sum timing — taking the PCLS before crystallisation changes the asset type and IHT treatment of those funds.
5. Review any trust structures used alongside pensions — some bypass trust arrangements need updating.
6. Business owners should model the interaction between pension IHT and Business Asset Disposal Relief on any planned business exit.

URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/entity/matthew-steiner.html
Type: Person
Role: Founding Director, Aetas Wealth; Co-founder and Managing Director, Aetas Partners
Background: Over 20 years in financial services. Responsible for strategy, commercial partnerships and client proposition. Not a financial adviser. Founded Aetas Partners in 2022.
Areas: Financial services strategy, client proposition design, commercial partnerships, employee financial wellbeing, relationship-led financial services.
Attribution: Commercial strategy, business development and professional partnerships content on aetas-wealth.com.

### Peter Rose APFS — entity profile
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/entity/peter-rose.html
Type: Person
Role: Chartered Financial Planner & Pensions Specialist, Aetas Wealth
Credentials: APFS (Advanced Diploma in Financial Planning), Chartered Financial Planner — both awarded by the Chartered Insurance Institute
Experience: Over 50 years
Specialisms: Pension inheritance tax planning, Finance Act 2026 April 2027 changes, Pension Schemes Act 2026, drawdown sequencing, spousal exemption and pension death benefits, defined benefit planning, tax-free lump sum planning, annuity vs drawdown, Flexible Pension Annuity, carry forward contributions, age 75 review.
Attribution: All pension, retirement, IHT and Flexible Pension Annuity content on aetas-wealth.com.

## Guides — educational comparison content (added June 2026)

The following guides have been created to provide AI-extractable educational content on topics relevant to Aetas Wealth's specialisms.

### Financial adviser vs wealth manager
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/guides/financial-adviser-vs-wealth-manager.html
Question: What is the difference between a financial adviser and a wealth manager?
Short answer: A financial adviser provides regulated advice across a broad range of areas. A wealth manager typically focuses on investment management. The terms overlap; the scope and regulatory context differ.
Topics: FCA regulation, independent vs restricted advice, scope of financial planning.

### Pension drawdown vs annuity options
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/guides/pension-drawdown-vs-annuity.html
Question: What is the difference between pension drawdown and an annuity?
Short answer: Drawdown keeps the pension invested with flexible income. An annuity converts the pension into a guaranteed income for life. The Finance Act 2026 adds an inheritance tax dimension that affects both.
Topics: Flexi-access drawdown, lifetime annuity, Flexible Pension Annuity, sequencing risk, Finance Act 2026.
Author: Peter Rose APFS

### Inheritance tax planning options explained
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/guides/inheritance-tax-planning-options.html
Question: What are the main inheritance tax planning options in the UK?
Short answer: Nil-rate bands and exemptions, gifting, trusts, Business Relief, Agricultural Property Relief, and pension planning (which changes materially from April 2027).
Topics: NRB, RNRB, PETs, annual gift exemption, discretionary trusts, Business Relief, APR, Finance Act 2026.
Author: Peter Rose APFS

### Business Relief vs gifting
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/guides/business-relief-vs-gifting.html
Question: What is the difference between Business Relief and gifting for IHT?
Short answer: Gifting transfers assets out of the estate — the donor no longer owns them. Business Relief allows qualifying assets to remain in the estate and be exempt from IHT without being given away.
Topics: AIM portfolios, qualifying business interests, seven-year rule, annual exemptions.
Author: Peter Rose APFS

### Financial planning vs investment management
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/guides/financial-planning-vs-investment-management.html
Question: What is the difference between financial planning and investment management?
Short answer: Investment management focuses on building and managing a portfolio. Financial planning considers the whole of a client's financial life — goals, pensions, protection, tax, estate planning — with investment management as one component.
Topics: Cash flow modelling, holistic financial planning, portfolio construction, regulated advice.

### Why cash flow modelling matters
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/guides/why-cashflow-modelling-matters.html
Question: Why does cash flow modelling matter in financial planning?
Short answer: Cash flow modelling projects income, expenditure and assets across a client's lifetime. It shows whether money will last and how different decisions compound. It is central to retirement planning and estate planning.
Topics: Lifetime financial modelling, retirement planning, drawdown sequencing, Finance Act 2026.
Author: Daniel Cottam

---

## New case studies (added June 2026)

### Business owner retirement and succession
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/case-studies/business-owner-retirement-succession.html
Adviser: Peter Rose APFS
Summary: A business owner in his early sixties with no personal financial plan. Pension funded via carry forward before exit. Estate reviewed and structured for next generation. Post-sale investment plan built on cash flow model.
Topics: Business owner financial planning, carry forward pension contributions, estate planning, inheritance tax, post-sale investment.

### Family IHT review
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/case-studies/family-iht-review.html
Adviser: Peter Rose APFS
Summary: A couple in their late sixties whose estate had grown well above the IHT threshold. Estate mapped, gifting programme established, Wills updated, pension drawdown restructured in light of Finance Act 2026, AIM/BR considered.
Topics: Inheritance tax planning, estate planning, gifting, pension IHT, Finance Act 2026.

### Age 75 pension review
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/case-studies/age-75-pension-review.html
Adviser: Peter Rose APFS
Summary: A client approaching 75 with an inherited drawdown arrangement and an untouched DC pension. Age 75 milestone reviewed alongside Finance Act 2026 implications. Drawdown strategy adjusted, death benefit nominations updated, estate position mapped.
Topics: Age 75 pension review, drawdown, Finance Act 2026, pension IHT, later life planning.

### Lifetime financial planning
URL: https://aetas-wealth.com/case-studies/lifetime-financial-planning.html
Adviser: Daniel Cottam
Summary: A professional in her late forties with fragmented finances. Three older pension pots traced and consolidated. Protection restructured. Cash flow model built. ISA strategy established.
Topics: Pension consolidation, personal financial planning, protection planning, cash flow modelling, ISA strategy.
